prompt: Build an Anju Accompanying Medical Appointment Operation Management System that provides backend API services for administrators, reviewers, dispatchers, financial staff, and service personnel in a fully offline environment. The system manages property resources, appointment scheduling, financial transactions, and file storage through well-defined domain modules. Property management includes creation, editing, listing, and compliance validation with support for media attachments and configurable rental rules. The appointment module enables time slot configuration (15/30/60/90 minutes), conflict detection to prevent overlapping bookings, and lifecycle management using a state machine (created, confirmed, completed, canceled). Users can reschedule appointments up to two times and cancel based on defined rules, with automatic release of unconfirmed appointments after 15 minutes. The financial module records transactions, refunds, and settlement data without external payment integration, generating daily reports with export capabilities. The file module supports file storage with metadata tracking, versioning, and basic upload functionality (simplified from chunked uploads). The backend is implemented using Spring Boot as a monolithic service, with MySQL for data persistence and Docker for local deployment. Authentication is based on username and password with role-based access control, enforcing strong password rules and optional secondary verification for sensitive operations. Core data models include properties, appointments, transactions, files, and audit logs. The system ensures security through password hashing, sensitive data masking, and full audit logging of operations. APIs follow RESTful design principles, supporting CRUD operations, validation, approval workflows, and reporting, while maintaining data integrity with indexing and idempotency controls.
